user_query:
Read the session transcript at input/transcript.md and the raw error output at input/errors.txt. Then create structured continuous-improvement logs in markdown under output/.learnings/ as follows:
- Append at least two learning entries to output/.learnings/LEARNINGS.md using the format with fields: ID, Logged, Priority, Status, Area, Summary, Details, Suggested Action, and a Metadata block (include Source, Related Files, Tags, See Also; include a Pattern-Key for any recurring pattern you identify). Use ID formats LRN-YYYYMMDD-XXX with a 3-digit or 3-char suffix.
- Append at least one error entry to output/.learnings/ERRORS.md using the error format (ID ERR-YYYYMMDD-XXX, Logged, Priority, Status, Area, Summary, a fenced code block under "### Error" containing the actual error from input/errors.txt, Context, Suggested Fix, Metadata including Reproducible and Related Files). Link this error to a related learning with See Also in both directions.
- Append at least one feature request entry to output/.learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md using the feature format (ID FEAT-YYYYMMDD-XXX, Logged, Priority, Status, Area, Requested Capability, User Context, Complexity Estimate, Suggested Implementation, Metadata including Frequency and Related Features). Base this on the user’s request in the transcript for better logging workflows.
- Promote any broadly applicable learning from the transcript (e.g., the correct package manager and install command) by: (1) distilling a concise rule into output/CLAUDE.md, and (2) updating the corresponding learning entry’s Status to "promoted" and adding a Promoted: CLAUDE.md note in its Metadata. Ensure the distilled rule is brief and actionable (e.g., a one-line convention with the correct command to use).
- Use the current date for ID prefixes and ISO-8601 timestamps for Logged. Do not modify files under input/. Write only to output/ paths specified above.
